title: बटुक भैरव स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री बटुक भैरव स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
वन्दे बालं भैरवं भूतनाथं
श्वेताङ्गं तं रक्तवस्त्रावृताङ्गम्।
त्र्यक्षं सौम्यं दण्डपाणिं त्रिशूलं
खट्वाङ्गं च डमरुं शूलहस्तम्॥

ॐ ह्रीं बटुकाय आपदुद्धारणाय कुरु कुरु बटुकाय ह्रीं ॐ नमः॥

॥ स्तोत्रम् ॥
बटुकाय नमस्तुभ्यं भैरवाय नमो नमः।
भूतनाथ नमस्तेऽस्तु भवभीति विनाशन॥ १॥

कालकालाय रुद्राय नीलकण्ठाय शम्भवे।
दिगम्बराय शुद्धाय क्षेत्रपालाय ते नमः॥ २॥

कपालमालाभरणं दंष्ट्राकरालवक्त्रकम्।
चतुर्भुजं त्रिनेत्रं च बटुकं तं नमाम्यहम्॥ ३॥

विकराळं महाकायं सर्पहारं सदाशिवम्।
भक्तानुग्रहकर्तारं बटुकं तं नमाम्यहम्॥ ४॥

खड्गं खट्वाङ्गमुण्डं च त्रिशूलं डमरुं तथा।
धारयन्तं महादेवं बटुकं तं नमाम्यहम्॥ ५॥

श्मशानवासिनं देवं गणनाथं गणेश्वरम्।
अष्टभैरवमुख्यं च बटुकं तं नमाम्यहम्॥ ६॥

योगिनीनां प्रियं देवं डाकिनीनां प्रपूजितम्।
शाकिनीनां वरं दाता बटुकं तं नमाम्यहम्॥ ७॥

अन्नपूर्णाप्रियं शान्तं वाराणस्यां प्रतिष्ठितम्।
कोटिसूर्यप्रभं देवं बटुकं तं नमाम्यहम्॥ ८॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
बटुकाष्टकमेतद्यः पठेद्भक्तिसमन्वितः।
न तस्य जायते भीतिर्नोपसर्गो न चापदः॥

सर्वव्याधिविनिर्मुक्तः सर्वरोगविवर्जितः।
सर्वसिद्धिमवाप्नोति बटुकस्य प्रसादतः॥

॥ इति श्री बटुक भैरव स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Batuk Bhairav Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Vande Balam Bhairavam Bhutanatham
Shvetangam Tam Raktavastraavritangam.
Tryaksham Saumyam Dandapaanim Trishulam
Khatvangam Cha Damarum Shulahastam.

Om Hrim Batukaya Aapad-uddharanaya Kuru Kuru Batukaya Hrim Om Namah.

|| Stotram ||
Batukaya Namastubhyam Bhairavaya Namo Namah.
Bhutanatha Namaste-astu Bhavabhiti Vinashana. || 1 ||

Kalakalaya Rudraya Nilakanthaya Shambhave.
Digambaraya Shuddhaya Kshetrapalaya Te Namah. || 2 ||

Kapalamalaa-bharanam Damshtraa-karala-vaktrakam.
Chaturbhujam Trinetram Cha Batukam Tam Namamyaham. || 3 ||

Vikaralam Mahakayam Sarpaharam Sadashivam.
Bhaktanugraha-kartaram Batukam Tam Namamyaham. || 4 ||

Khadgam Khatvanga-mundam Cha Trishulam Damarum Tatha.
Dharayantam Mahadevam Batukam Tam Namamyaham. || 5 ||

Shmashana-vasinam Devam Gananatham Ganeshwaram.
Ashtabhairava-mukhyam Cha Batukam Tam Namamyaham. || 6 ||

Yogininam Priyam Devam Dakininam Prapujitam.
Shakininam Varam Data Batukam Tam Namamyaham. || 7 ||

Annapurna-priyam Shantam Varanasyam Pratishthitam.
Kotisuryaprabham Devam Batukam Tam Namamyaham. || 8 ||

|| Phalashruti ||
Batukaashtakam-etad-yah Pathed-bhakti-samanvitah.
Na Tasya Jayate Bhitir-nopasargo Na Chaapadah.

Sarva-vyadhi-vinirmuktah Sarva-roga-vivarjitah.
Sarva-siddhim-avaapnoti Batukasya Prasadatah.

|| Iti Shri Batuk Bhairav Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
इस स्तोत्र में बटुक भैरव (भगवान शिव के बाल भैरव रूप) की स्तुति है। भैरव भगवान शिव के भयानक किन्तु कृपालु स्वरूप हैं जो भक्तों की समस्त भय और आपदाओं से रक्षा करते हैं।

वे कालों के भी काल हैं, रुद्र हैं, नीलकण्ठ हैं, दिगम्बर हैं और क्षेत्रपाल हैं। वे कपालमाला, खड्ग, खट्वाङ्ग, मुण्ड, त्रिशूल और डमरु धारण करते हैं। वे अन्नपूर्णा देवी के प्रिय हैं और काशी (वाराणसी) में प्रतिष्ठित हैं।

जो व्यक्ति भक्तिपूर्वक इस स्तोत्र का पाठ करता है, उसे कभी भय, उपसर्ग और आपदा नहीं होती। वह समस्त रोगों से मुक्त होकर बटुक भैरव की कृपा से सर्वसिद्धि प्राप्त करता है।
benefits: Provides powerful protection from all dangers, fears, and calamities
Traditionally recited for removal of evil eye, black magic, and negative energies
Invokes fearlessness and courage in difficult situations
Traditionally invoked for protection from enemies and adversarial forces
Invokes the blessing of all siddhis by the grace of batuk bhairav
Especially powerful when recited at Kala Bhairav temples in Varanasi
description: Dedicated to Lord Bhairava, the Batuk Bhairav Stotram (बटुक भैरव स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. This revered text originates from Rudrayamala Tantra. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to protect from enemies and adversarial forces as well as bestow all siddhis by the grace of Batuk Bhairav. Additionally, it is known to remove evil eye, black magic, and negative energies. It is especially recommended on Sunday (Ravivar) and Tuesday (Mangalvar) and during Kalashtami and Maha Shivaratri for maximum spiritual benefit. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 8 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Batuk Bhairav Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Batuk Bhairav Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning and transliteration. Powerful Bhairav prayer for protection from fears and calamities.
relatedSearches: Batuk Bhairav Stotram lyrics in Sanskrit Hindi PDF
Batuk Bhairav mantra Om Hreem Batukaya Apaduddharanaya
Batuk Bhairav puja vidhi Tuesday Sunday night
Bal Bhairav child form temples India
Apaduddharaka Batuk Bhairav for crisis relief
Batuk Bhairav Kashi Kala Bhairava difference

seoFaqs.question: What is the Batuk Bhairav Stotram and what does it invoke?
seoFaqs.answer: The Batuk Bhairav Stotram is a Sanskrit Tantric hymn invoking Batuk Bhairav (also spelled Batuka Bhairava) — the child or youthful form (batuka = young boy) of Lord Bhairava, the fierce manifestation of Shiva. While Kala Bhairava is the terrifying mature form, Batuk Bhairav is depicted as a five-year-old boy with a beautiful face, holding a danda (staff), accompanied by a black dog (his vahana), and wearing minimal ornaments — the most accessible and quickly responsive form of Bhairava for devotees. The stotra is preserved in the Rudrayamala Tantra and the Batuka Bhairava Kalpa, and is one of the most recited stotras in North Indian Tantric traditions for crisis relief (apaduddharaka). (Sources: Rudrayamala Tantra; Batuka Bhairava Kalpa; Hindupedia Bhairava.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Batuk Bhairav and how does he differ from Kala Bhairav?
seoFaqs.answer: Batuk Bhairav is the swift-acting child-form of Bhairava, said to manifest in less than the time it takes to complete one round of japa, hence his epithet Apaduddharaka — the swift remover of crisis. Where Kala Bhairav is the mature, fierce kotwal of Kashi who governs death and time, Batuk Bhairav is the playful but equally powerful child-Bhairava who responds especially to children, simple devotees, and those in immediate danger. Tradition holds that one should approach Kala Bhairav for the dissolution of long-standing karmic obstructions and Batuk Bhairav for immediate worldly emergencies. The Tantra prescribes the Batuk-mantra (Om Hreem Batukaya Apaduddharanaya Kuru Kuru Batukaya Hreem) for both. (Sources: Rudrayamala Tantra; Batuka Bhairava Kalpa; Hindupedia Bhairava forms.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Apaduddharaka Batuk Bhairav?
seoFaqs.answer: Apaduddharaka (apad = crisis, calamity; uddharaka = lifter, rescuer) is Batuk Bhairav's most invoked epithet, meaning the one who lifts the devotee out of any sudden crisis. Tantric tradition prescribes the Batuk Bhairav Stotram and his ashtottara as the first response to any acute emergency: serious illness, accident, persecution, theft, false accusation, snake-bite, fire, drowning, hostile spirits, or sudden financial collapse. The promise is that Batuk Bhairav, being the swift child-form, responds within hours rather than days. The Apaduddharaka Batuk Bhairav Stotram with its 32 names is recited 108 times during severe crises, often through the night, with offerings of urad-dal khichdi to a black dog (Bhairava's vahana). (Sources: Apaduddharaka Stotra; Rudrayamala Tantra; Hindupedia Bhairava upasana.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Batuk Bhairav Stotram be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The most powerful occasions are Bhairava Ashtami (Margashirsha Krishna Ashtami), every Kalashtami, Tuesday and Sunday nights (especially after midnight, the Bhairava-yama), Rahu-Kala period of any day, and during personal crises. The recitation should be done facing south or south-west, before a Batuk Bhairav image or photograph, with a deepa of mustard oil or sesame oil, offerings of red flowers (especially red hibiscus), urad-dal khichdi, jalebi, sweets, country liquor (or coconut water/panchamrita as substitute for Vaishnava sadhakas), and feeding a black dog before commencing. The minimum daily recitation is 11 times; for crisis 108 times for 21 or 41 days. (Sources: Batuka Bhairava Kalpa; Drikpanchang Bhairava Ashtami; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of the dog as Batuk Bhairav's vahana?
seoFaqs.answer: The black dog (shvana) is the vahana (vehicle) of Bhairava in all his forms, including Batuk Bhairav. Tantric tradition holds that feeding a black dog with milk, jalebi, or urad-dal khichdi is direct seva to Bhairava himself, and particularly potent when done before reciting the Batuk Bhairav Stotram. Kashi tradition holds that no Bhairava worship is complete without honouring a black dog, and Kala Bhairava temples maintain resident dogs that are revered as Bhairava-prasada. The dog symbolises Bhairava's loyal protection of dharma, his presence at burial grounds (his preferred haunt), and his ability to perceive what humans cannot — including approaching evil and the moment of death. (Sources: Rudrayamala Tantra; Kashi Khanda; Hindupedia Bhairava iconography.)

seoFaqs.question: Where are the major Batuk Bhairav temples in India?
seoFaqs.answer: Major Batuk Bhairav shrines include the Batuk Bhairav temple at Banaras Hindu University (Varanasi), the Batuk Bhairav temple within the Kala Bhairava complex at Kashi, the Bal Bhairav temples in Maharashtra (notably at Pune and Nashik), the Batuk Bhairav shrines at Ujjain (alongside Kala Bhairav), the Batuk Bhairav at Pashupatinath in Kathmandu, and several Tantric peethas in Bengal and Assam where Batuk Bhairav is the kshetra-pala of Shakti shrines. Devotees offer Tuesday and Sunday night abhishekam, recite the Batuk Bhairav Stotram during the puja, and feed black dogs in the temple courtyard. (Sources: Hindupedia Bhairava temples; Kashi Khanda; Maharashtra HRCE temple records.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Batuk Bhairav Stotram promise to its reciters?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition holds that regular recitation grants the swift removal of any sudden crisis (apaduddharana), protection from accidents and untimely death, freedom from black magic, evil spirits, ghost possession and tantric attacks (abhichara-nashana), recovery of lost or stolen property, success in litigation and conflict, removal of debts, protection during night travel and dangerous journeys, healing from acute illnesses and snake-bites, removal of obstacles in childbirth and progeny, and the cultivation of fearlessness (abhaya). For children, the stotra is regarded as especially protective since Batuk Bhairav is the child-form himself. The Tantra promises that no recitation of this stotra ever goes unanswered. (Sources: Batuka Bhairava Kalpa phalashruti; Rudrayamala Tantra; Hindupedia Bhairava upasana.)
